title: Guineo
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Guineo.jpg]] |name=Guineo |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height=Over 2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Pale yellow]]''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' |haircolor=None |feathers= |eyecolor=Red''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', [[:File:Guineo.jpg|Guineo image]] |distinctions= |lifespan= |origin=[[Dohlban]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|You don't have to get mean. I'm new. They don't tell me much.|A Guineo guard, to his Draag supervisor|}} The '''Guineo''' were a bipedal, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dohlban]], where they were often used for manual labor. Typically, members of the species were tall and powerfully built. Guineo, however, were not very intelligent and lacked the ability to make complicated decisions, a condition that relegated many Guineo to very basic jobs. Many members of the species were hired off-world by criminal organizations. Among these groups was [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], which was located in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Several Guineo worked at the company, a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-meat packing plant that was used as a cover for transporting [[Hallucination/Legends|hallucinogenic]] [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]] from the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sebolto]] on the planet [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] so that they could be sold on Coruscant. Among the Guineo working at Haugg Nerf Industries were [[Yol Areebi]], [[J'Meeb Gumb]], and [[Panza Hondi]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|It's cold in here!|A Guineo guard at Haugg Nerf Industries|}} The Guineo were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], bipedal [[Species/Legends|species]] with [[Color/Legends|pale-yellow]] skin and two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] that ended in large, four-fingered hands. They were large and powerfully built, with the average Guineo being over two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall on legs that were shorter than the arms. Each member of the species had a tall, elongated, [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] head, a thick, wrinkled neck, and two red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on the face. The Guineo also had a large [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] and two small, sharply-pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]], one on each side of the head. The Guineo species included a male [[Gender/Legends|sex]]. There was at least one [[Unidentified Guineo guard|Guineo]] who found cold [[Temperature/Legends|temperatures]] unpleasant. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|It ain't the nerfs he's got us guarding, you idiot!|A Draag supervisor, demeaning the intelligence of a Guineo guard at Haugg Nerf Industries|}} The Guineo, as far as sentient species went, were one of the least intelligent in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Their meager intelligence made them largely unable to make complex decisions, limiting the Guineo to simple jobs. Guineo were able to learn how to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Many Guineo workers who lived in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] wore [[Combat jumpsuit|jumpsuits]], each having a large metallic plate on its front. Their jumpsuits had two tubes connecting to both the upper chest area and to a small tank that they carried on their backs. Such Guineo guards also wore [[Glove/Legends|gloves]] and [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]]. ==History== {{Quote|What are we guarding, anyway?''"
"''Death sticks.''"
"''Death sticks?|A Guineo guard at Haugg Nerf Industries, asking his Draag supervisor about their guard duty|}} The Guineo species was native to the planet [[Dohlban]], which was located in the [[Dohlban system/Legends|Dohlban system]] in the [[Dohlbani sector/Legends|Dohlbani sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} The [[Star system/Legends|star system]] lay along the [[Kaaga Run]] past the [[Void Station system/Legends|system]] in which [[Void Station]] was located.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Guineo in the galaxy== {{Quote|Why we gotta stand guard in here? The boss afraid somebody's going to steal a bunch of frozen nerfs?|A Guineo guard, to his Draag supervisor|}} [[File:Duginlikeanalabamatick.jpg|thumb|left|120px|A Guineo criminal named Panza Hondi]] On their homeworld of Dohlban, the Guineo worked as manual laborers, but some were hired as muscle by criminal organizations from other worlds. In any case, members of the Guineo species generally had very basic jobs due to their poor decision-making skills. Some Guineo worked in the Industrial District of the galactic capital planet of Coruscant in the year [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The company [[Haugg Nerf Industries]] was located in that district and employed members of the Guineo species as guards. While Haugg Nerf Industries posed as a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-meat processing facility owned by a non-Guineo [[Alien/Legends|alien]] named [[Groff Haugg]], in reality the plant was a front for selling [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]] shipped from the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sebolto]] of the planet [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] to people on Coruscant. Among the many Guineo working in the facility were [[Yol Areebi]], [[J'Meeb Gumb]], [[Panza Hondi]], and a newly-hired guard whose job was to watch over a [[Airspeeder/Legends|speeder]] [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where [[Death/Legends|death]] sticks were stored before being distributed. Areebi had previously worked as a [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] and had earned a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] from the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] for supplying an illegal [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]] [[Unidentified Cron Drift mining facility|mining facility]] with natives from the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Gumb was wanted by an anonymous source for using a [[Skifter/Legends|skifter]] to manipulate the deck in a high-stakes [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] match. The [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police]] wanted Hondi for the [[Death/Legends|murder]] of one of their [[Unidentified Coruscant Police officer (Panza Hondi)|police officers]]. In 32 BBY, a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrated]] Haugg Nerf Industries to get information from the owner, [[Groff Haugg]], about the origin of the death sticks. When he entered Haugg Nerf through the hangar, Fett overheard the newly hired guard and his [[Draag]] [[Unidentified Draag supervisor|supervisor]] discussing the operation. As Fett progressed through the facility, he was attacked by guards, including many Guineo armed with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] or [[thermal wrench]]es. While inside Haugg Nerf, the bounty hunter encountered Hondi and claimed the bounty on his head.{{CSWECite|II|55|Hondi, Panza}} {{Gamemechanics}} Besides capturing Hondi on one of the facility's walkways, Fett captured J'Meeb Gumb near a control panel and apprehended Yol Areebi in a corridor shortly before locating Groff Haugg, who had been killed by another Mandalorian bounty hunter named [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]]. {{Endgame}} ==Behind the scenes== The first official image of a member of the Guineo species was published in [[April]] [[2002]] on the cover of [[Star Wars Gamer 10|''Star Wars Gamer'' 10]] to advertise a preview [[State of the Arts|article]] for the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''.[[Star Wars Gamer 10|''Star Wars Gamer'' 10]] When ''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'' was released in [[December]] 2002, Guineo appeared as guards in Haugg Nerf Industries, which was the second stage of the game's second level. One Guineo guard appears prominently in a cutscene before the start of the stage, which features the guard and a Draag supervisor having a conversation about what they were guarding in the facility while Jango Fett sneaked in. In addition to the standard guards, three of the Guineo from the level have secondary bounties placed on them, which Fett, the player's character, has the opportunity to collect. These three Guineo are Yol Areebi, J'Meeb Gumb, and Panza Hondi. Each of the Guineo bounties offer twice as many [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] alive as the reward for killing the bounty. Gumb and Hondi fight Fett if attacked, while Areebi merely cowers and allows himself to be captured if he is approached. All of the Guineo on the level use the same character model, including the three bounties. This article was written with the assumption that the player collects every bounty at the highest possible reward for each bounty, but the only Guineo bounty that Jango Fett is [[canon]]ically confirmed to have collected was Hondi. This information on Hondi was included in his entry in the [[2008]] book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', although it did not confirm if Fett captures him alive or kills him. While game artwork for the Guineo shows the species with eyes that appear red in color, the graphics used for the in-game model make it impossible to determine if they have red eyes or merely red-tinted lenses on their goggles. This article was written with the assumption that the artwork is correct, since the image was of a higher quality than that of the in-game Guineo model. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Guineo}} *[[Star Wars Gamer 10|''Star Wars Gamer'' 10]] {{1stp}} {{C|Cover}} *[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{CSWECite|I||Hondi, Panza}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Guineo#legends |pl=Guineo}} [[Category:Mid Rim sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Guineo]] }}
